free_text: Single-particle structure of silicon isotopes
approaching 42Si
The experiment was performed at the
Coupled Cyclotron Facility at the National
Superconducting Cyclotron Laboratory at Michigan
State University
Reaction products were momentum analysed using
S800 spectrograph and identified by detectors placed
in spectrograph focal plane
The position and angle of the residues were
measured using two cathode-readout drift chambers in
focal plane
Time of flight was measured using plastic
scintillators in beam line
Prompt gamma rays were detected with the
GRETINA array, surrounding the target position of the
S800. Doppler reconstruction was performed on an
event-by-event basis.
The trajectory of the particle was traced back to the
target using the ion optics code COSY and yielding
the momentum vector and nondispersive position at the
target. Heavy reaction residues were identified using
time of flight and momentum information
Secondary beams of 36Si,38Si and 40Si were produced
by fragmentation of a 140MeV/u 48Ca primary beam
interacting with a 9Be production target and purified
in the A1900 fragment separator
No information given
Compiled by S.H.
